title: Spinal cord stimulation system determining optimal sub-perception therapy by using neural dose
abstract: Methods and systems for determining sub-perception stimulation for a patient having a spinal cord stimulator device are disclosed. In one example, an external device includes an algorithm configured to determine a stimulation program for the stimulator device. The algorithm includes a model that comprises pre-determined energy values that cause sub-perception stimulation. The algorithm is configured to determine stimulation parameters for the stimulation program that yield an energy value within the first model. The energy values in the model may be expressed as a function of frequency. The model in particular provides optimal sub-perception stimulation at low frequencies, such as at 1 kHz and below, or even at 400 Hz and below.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A system, comprising: a spinal cord stimulator device comprising a plurality of electrodes, wherein the stimulator device is programmable to provide stimulation to a patientâs tissue at at least one of the plurality of electrodes in accordance with a stimulation program; and an external device configured to program the stimulator device with the stimulation program, wherein the external device is programmed with an algorithm configured to determine the stimulation program in accordance with a first model, wherein the first model comprises pre-determined energy values that cause sub-perception stimulation, wherein the algorithm is configured to determine stimulation parameters for the stimulation program that yield an energy value within the first model. 2. The system of claim 1, wherein the energy values in first model comprise mean charge per second values. 3. The system of claim 1, wherein the first model comprises the energy values represented as a function of values of one of the stimulation parameters. 4. The system of claim 3, wherein the first model comprises a mathematical relationship expressing the energy values as a function of one of the stimulation parameters. 5. The system of claim 3, wherein the one of the stimulation parameters comprises frequency. 6. The system of claim 5, wherein the frequencies in the first model are equal to or lower than 1 kHz. 7. The system of claim 5, wherein the frequencies in the first model are equal to or lower than 400 Hz. 8. The system of claim 3, wherein the first model includes a range of the energy values at each of the values of the one of the stimulation parameters. 9. The system of claim 1, wherein the algorithm is further configured to determine the stimulation program in accordance with a second model, wherein the second model comprises information relating frequencies and pulse widths that cause sub-perception stimulation. 10. The system of claim 9, wherein the algorithm selects a frequency and pulse width as two of the stimulation parameters in accordance with the second model, and selects an amplitude as another stimulation parameter to yield the energy value within the first model. 11. The system of claim 1, wherein the stimulation parameters for the stimulation program comprise amplitude, pulse width, and frequency. 12. The system of claim 1, wherein the first model comprises pre-determined information relating the energy values and frequencies values that cause sub-perception stimulation. 13. The system of claim 12, wherein the first model comprises the energy values and frequency values that are on or within one or more linearly -bounded regions defined by points: (i) (10 Hz, 6 pC/s), (10 Hz, 12 pC/s), (50 Hz, 55 pC/s), and (50 Hz, 27 pC/s); or (ii) (50 Hz, 27 pC/s), (50 Hz, 55 pC/s), (100 Hz, 88 pC/s), and (100 Hz, 40 pC/s); or (iii) (100 Hz, 40 pC/s), (100 Hz, 88 pC/s), (200 Hz, 151 pC/s), and (200 Hz, 67 pC/s); or (iv) (200 Hz, 67 pC/s), (200 Hz, 151 pC/s), (400 Hz, 274 pC/s), and (400 Hz, 118 pC/s); or (v) (400 Hz, 118 pC/s), (400 Hz, 274 pC/s), (600 Hz, 357 pC/s), (600 Hz, 141 pC/s); or (vi) (600 Hz, 141 pC/s), (600 Hz, 357 pC/s), (1000 Hz, 471 pC/s), (1000 Hz, 181 pC/s). 14. The system of claim 12, wherein the first model comprises the energy values and frequency values that are on or within one or more linearly -bounded regions defined by points: (i) (10 Hz, 6 pC/s), (10 Hz, 12 pC/s), (50 Hz, 55 pC/s), and (50 Hz, 27 pC/s); or (ii) (50 Hz, 27 pC/s), (50 Hz, 55 pC/s), (100 Hz, 88 pC/s), and (100 Hz, 40 pC/s); or (iii) (100 Hz, 40 pC/s), (100 Hz, 88 pC/s), (200 Hz, 151 pC/s), and (200 Hz, 67 pC/s); or (iv) (200 Hz, 67 pC/s), (200 Hz, 151 pC/s), (400 Hz, 274 pC/s), and (400 Hz, 118 pC/s). 15. The system of claim 1, wherein the spinal cord stimulator is programmable during a programming session, and wherein the sub-perception stimulation is washed in within a period of one hour or less during the programming session. 16. The system of claim 1, wherein the spinal cord stimulator is programmable during a programming session, and wherein the sub-perception stimulation is washed in within a period of ten minutes or less during the programming session. 17. The system of claim 1, wherein the external device is configured to cause the stimulator device to cease providing the sub-perception stimulation, wherein effectiveness of the sub-perception stimulation occurs during a wash out period of ten minutes or greater after the sub-perception stimulation has ceased. 18. The system of claim 1, wherein the external device is configured to cause the stimulator device to cease providing the sub-perception stimulation, wherein effectiveness of the sub-perception stimulation occurs during a wash out period of one hour or greater after the sub-perception stimulation has ceased. 19. A spinal cord stimulator device, comprising: a plurality of electrodes insertable within a patientâs spinal column; and control circuitry programmed to provide sub-perception stimulation pulses to a patientâs tissue at at least one of the plurality of electrodes in accordance with a stimulation program defined by a plurality of stimulation parameters 

 including pulse frequency, wherein the stimulation parameters comprise an energy and a frequency that is on or within one or more linearly-bounded regions defined by points: (i) (10 Hz, 6 pC/s), (10 Hz, 12 pC/s), (50 Hz, 55 pC/s), and (50 Hz, 27 pC/s); or (ii) (50 Hz, 27 pC/s), (50 Hz, 55 pC/s), (100 Hz, 88 pC/s), and (100 Hz, 40 pC/s); or (iii) (100 Hz, 40 pC/s), (100 Hz, 88 pC/s), (200 Hz, 151 pC/s), and (200 Hz, 67 pC/s); or (iv) (200 Hz, 67 pC/s), (200 Hz, 151 pC/s), (400 Hz, 274 pC/s), and (400 Hz, 118 pC/s). 20. The spinal cord stimulator device of claim 19, wherein the control circuitry is configured to provide the stimulation pulses at a constant current amplitude. 21. The spinal cord stimulator device of claim 19, wherein each pulse is followed by a charge recovery pulse phase. 22. The spinal cord stimulator device of claim 19, wherein the spinal cord stimulator is programmable during a programming session, and wherein the sub-perception stimulation pulses are washed in within a period of one hour or less during the programming session. 23. The spinal cord stimulator device of claim 19, wherein the spinal cord stimulator is programmable during a programming session, and wherein the sub-perception stimulation pulses are washed in within a period of ten minutes or less during the programming session. 24. The spinal cord stimulator device of claim 19, wherein the control circuitry is further configured to cease providing the sub-perception stimulation pulses, wherein effectiveness of the sub-perception stimulation pulses occurs during a wash out period of ten minutes or greater after the sub-perception stimulation pulses have ceased. 25. The spinal cord stimulator device of claim 19, wherein the control circuitry is further configured to cease providing the sub-perception stimulation pulses, wherein effectiveness of 

 the sub-perception stimulation pulses occurs during a wash out period of one hour or greater after the sub-perception stimulation pulses have ceased. 26. A spinal cord stimulator device, comprising: a plurality of electrodes insertable within a patientâs spinal column; and control circuitry programmed to provide sub-perception stimulation pulses to a patientâs tissue at at least one of the plurality of electrodes in accordance with a stimulation program defined by a plurality of stimulation parameters including a frequency, a current amplitude, and a pulse width that is on or within one or more linearly-bounded volumes defined by points (in Hz, mA, ps): (i) (10, 1.73, 326), (10, 1.73, 374), (10, 3.39, 326), (10, 3.39, 374), (50, 1.65, 283), (50, 1.65, 333), (50, 3.43, 283), (50, 3.43, 333); or (ii) (50, 1.65, 283), (50, 1.65, 333), (50, 3.43, 283), (50, 3.43, 333), (100, 1.82, 233), (100, 1.82, 271), (100, 3.42, 233), (100, 3.42, 271); or (iii) (100, 1.82, 233), (100, 1.82, 271), (100, 3.42, 233), (100, 3.42, 271), (200, 1.66, 181), (200, 1.66, 237), (200, 3.52, 181), (200, 3.52, 237); or (iv) (200, 1.66, 181), (200, 1.66, 237), (200, 3.52, 181), (200, 3.52, 237), (400, 1.76, 162), (400, 1.76, 204), (400, 3.48, 162), (400, 3.48, 204). 27. The spinal cord stimulator device of claim 26, wherein the control circuitry is configured to provide the stimulation pulses at a constant current amplitude. 28. The spinal cord stimulator device of claim 26, wherein each pulse is followed by a charge recovery pulse phase. 29. The spinal cord stimulator device of claim 26, wherein the spinal cord stimulator is programmable during a programming session, and wherein the sub-perception stimulation pulses are washed in within a period of one hour or less during the programming session. 30. The spinal cord stimulator device of claim 26, wherein the spinal cord stimulator is programmable during a programming session, and wherein the sub-perception stimulation pulses are washed in within a period of ten minutes or less during the programming session. 31. The spinal cord stimulator device of claim 26, wherein the control circuitry is further configured to cease providing the sub-perception stimulation pulses, wherein effectiveness of the sub-perception stimulation pulses occurs during a wash out period of ten minutes or greater after the sub-perception stimulation pulses have ceased. 32. The spinal cord stimulator device of claim 26, wherein the control circuitry is further configured to cease providing the sub-perception stimulation pulses, wherein effectiveness of the sub-perception stimulation pulses occurs during a wash out period of one hour or greater after the sub-perception stimulation pulses have ceased.
